column1: Luis Arraez leads the pack tonight, setting the table atop Cincinnati’s lineup with his signature steady bat and a strong platoon edge against Rhett Lowder—no one’s more reliable for a hit right now. Nico Hoerner isn’t far behind, also locked into the leadoff spot with a favorable matchup and a nearly identical hit rate, making him a sharp alternative for Beat The Streak players. Trea Turner and Xavier Edwards both bring top-of-order roles and solid contact skills, but Arraez’s consistency and matchup make him the clear-cut pick to extend your streak.
